checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit=853600000 on SalesRevenueGoodsNet=2214300000 (Q ended 2011-02-28), margin ~38.6% while revenue declined from prior quarter
InventoryNet=816900000 (2010-11-30) rose to InventoryNet=1133600000 (2011-02-28), a 38.8% increase, even as SalesRevenueGoodsNet fell from 2674800000 to 2214300000 over the same period

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=100600000 (Q ended 2009-08-31); NetIncomeLoss=107800000 (Q ended 2009-11-30); NetIncomeLoss=222600000 (Q ended 2010-02-28); NetIncomeLoss=297700000 (Q ended 2010-08-31); NetIncomeLoss=1025600000 (Q ended 2010-11-30); NetIncomeLoss=542100000 (Q ended 2011-02-28)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?
Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-Q filed 2010-10-12 followed by 10-Q/A filed 2010-10-29 for the same period (2010-08-31), an amendment filed only 17 days after the original

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Balance sheet data (ReceivablesNetCurrent, InventoryNet, Goodwill, etc.) only spans four instant dates: 2010-05-31, 2010-08-31, 2010-11-30, 2011-02-28
NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities data provided for only two quarterly periods: 2009-06-01/2009-08-31 and 2010-06-01/2010-08-31

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential aggressive revenue recognition or delayed cash collection causing receivables to build disproportionately as revenue decelerated/declined, which could indicate channel stuffing, extended customer terms, or delayed write-offs of doubtful accounts rather than organic sales growth.

rationale_evidence.quote: ReceivablesNetCurrent=777900000 (2011-02-28) up from ReceivablesNetCurrent=553900000 (2010-08-31), a ~40% increase over two quarters
SalesRevenueGoodsNet=2214300000 (Q ended 2011-02-28) down from SalesRevenueGoodsNet=2674800000 (Q ended 2010-11-30), a ~17% decline

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible under-recognition or deferral of cost of goods sold (e.g., inventory costing/valuation adjustments) coinciding with a sharp gross margin expansion (23.1%→28.7%→38.6%) even as revenue declined quarter-over-quarter, alongside a simultaneous inventory build, which is atypical and could reflect capitalization of costs into inventory rather than expensing them.

rationale_evidence.quote: GrossProfit=853600000 on SalesRevenueGoodsNet=2214300000 (Q ended 2011-02-28), implying ~38.6% gross margin, up from ~28.7% in the prior quarter (GrossProfit=768300000 on SalesRevenueGoodsNet=2674800000, Q ended 2010-11-30) despite falling revenue
InventoryNet=1133600000 (2011-02-28) vs InventoryNet=816900000 (2010-11-30), a 38.8% sequential increase during a revenue decline

affected_line_items: Multiple balance sheet and income statement line items restated in 10-Q/A
mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: The company filed a 10-Q/A only 17 days after the original 10-Q for the quarter ended 2010-08-31, indicating a correction to previously issued financial statements; while the specific corrected line items are not detailed in the provided data, such rapid amendments typically reflect either an error identified post-filing or a reclassification, raising control-quality concerns even if not a large-dollar restatement.

top_signals: Receivables grew ~40% over two quarters (ReceivablesNetCurrent=553900000 to 777900000) while revenue declined ~17% quarter-over-quarter (SalesRevenueGoodsNet=2674800000 to 2214300000)
Gross margin expanded sharply from ~23.1% to ~38.6% across three quarters even as revenue fell, accompanied by a ~39% sequential inventory build (InventoryNet=816900000 to 1133600000)
A 10-Q/A amendment was filed just 17 days after the original 10-Q for the quarter ended 2010-08-31, and the filing history shows multiple historical NT 10-Q/NT 10-K late-filing notices
Provided data coverage is short (roughly 4 balance-sheet quarters, 6 income-statement quarters, only 2 operating cash flow quarters), limiting the ability to assess persistence of trends
